entries.definition: An easement appurtenant involves two pieces of land, where one serves as the servient tenement (estate) that bears the burden, and the other the dominant tenement (estate), which benefits from the grant of the easement and with which the easement passes as an appurtenance. Easements may be classified as affirmative

An affirmative easement gives the easement holder the right to do something on the grantor's land, such as travel on a road through the grantor's land. A negative easement gives the easement holder the right to prevent the owner of the land subject to the easement from doing something on his land that may otherwise be lawful for him to do, such as

A written real estate instrument that conveys a possessory interest in real property, usually exclusive, for a period of time

A lease carries a present interest and estate in the land for the period specified. The estate of the lessee, or tenant, is called the term and the estate of the lessor, or landlord, is the reversion. Generally, the lessee may occupy and use the premises for any lawful purpose not injurious to the reversion. However, the lease may contain express provisions or conditions restricting the use of the property. Leases are not appropriate between the Army and another DoD
